title: aster cordifolius subsp. laevigatus
intro: Aster cordifolius subsp. laevigatus, also known as the mountain or heart-leaved aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and wounds, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote healing. Herbal preparations of Aster cordifolius subsp. laevigatus include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
